concepts.title: Definition of Globalisation
concepts.description: Globalisation is the increasing interconnectedness of the world's economies, societies, and cultures due to the reduction of trade barriers and the emergence of new technologies.

concepts.title: Types of Globalisation
concepts.description: There are three types of globalisation: Economic Globalisation, Cultural Globalisation, and Political Globalisation.

concepts.title: Characteristics of Globalisation
concepts.description: Characteristics of Globalisation include Increased Economic Interdependence, Rise of Multinational Corporations, and the Emergence of a Global Culture.

concepts.title: Advantages of Globalisation
concepts.description: Advantages of Globalisation include Increased Economic Growth, Improved Living Standards, and Access to New Markets and Technologies.

concepts.title: What is the difference between Globalisation and Imperialism?
concepts.description: Globalisation is the increasing interconnectedness of the world's economies, societies, and cultures, while Imperialism is the extension of a country's power and influence through colonization or economic domination.

concepts.title: Globalisation Timeline
concepts.description: Use the mnemonic 'CAGE' to remember the key events in the history of globalisation: C - Colonisation, A - Asian Miracle, G - Globalisation, E - Economic Crisis.

concepts.title: Disadvantages of Globalisation
concepts.description: Disadvantages of Globalisation include Increased Inequality, Environmental Degradation, and the Loss of National Sovereignty.

concepts.title: Impact of Globalisation on Local Cultures
concepts.description: Globalisation can lead to the homogenisation of cultures, the loss of traditional practices, and the emergence of a global culture that threatens local identities.
